notes.note: Cn. Sallustius, a close friend of Cicero (not the historian); here he keeps urging Cicero to mediate the quarrel between Atticus and Lucceius.
L. Lucceius, a wealthy senator and historian on intimate terms with Cicero; he had fallen out with Atticus, and Cicero's failed attempt to reconcile them runs through letters 1.8, 1.10 and 1.11.

Designatus: a candidate who has won election but not yet entered office. Atticus had assumed Cicero's aedilician canvass was as good as won; Cicero replies that the elections are in chaos and not even scheduled.
A courier or agent (the Greek name suggests a freedman or slave) carrying news between Cicero and Atticus; otherwise unknown.
Cicero's name for a colonnaded walk at his Tusculan villa, modelled on Plato's Academy at Athens; he is impatient for the Greek art and books Atticus has gathered to furnish it.
